title: SACRAMENTO –The Senate Health Committee today approved a measure to create the first statewide Office of Suicide Prevention on an 8 to 0 vote.

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The novel coronavirus crisis has added new urgency to suicide prevention efforts.",
    "More than 65 percent increase in calls and emails has been reported by the National Alliance on Mental Illness HelpLine since March.",
    "Suicide is the second leading cause of death among young people ages 15 to 24 according to a 2017 study from the CDC.",
    "One in six high school students reported seriously considering ending their lives, based on CDC data from 1991-2017.",
    "More than one in 12 high school students reported attempted suicides according to the CDC.",
    "3,575 children aged five to 20 were hospitalized for non-fatal self-inflicted injuries in California in 2014.",
    "Tribal young people are particularly vulnerable to suicide but possess the strength and resiliency to overcome this crisis if engaged."
  ]
}
```
